name: Broiled Salmon with Curried Eggplant Chutney
recipeIngredient: 1 small red bell pepper, cut into 1/2-inch pieces
1/4 cup + 1 tablespoon firmly packed light brown sugar
1 tablespoon chopped fresh flat-leafed parsley leaves
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan, simmer eggplant, bell pepper, and onion in water, lemon juice, and cider vinegar with curry powder, mustard seeds, and 1/4 cup brown sugar, uncovered, stirring occasionally for about 20 minutes, or until most of the liquid is evaporated. Season the mixture with salt and stir in parsley. Cool chutney to room temperature.
2. Chutney may be made up to one week ahead and chilled, covered.
3. Preheat the broiler.
4. In a bowl, stir together balsamic vinegar, mustard, cayenne, remaining tablespoon brown sugar, and salt to taste. Add salmon, turning to coat evenly.
5. Arrange fish, skin side down, on the rack in the broiler pan about 4 inches from the heat. Broil for about 7 minutes, or until just cooked through. Discard the skin.
6. Serve fish with chutney and haricots verts and potatoes.
Note: Each serving with Haricots Verts and Potatoes contains approximately 377 calories and 8 grams of fat (19% of calories from fat). Each serving without Haricots Verts and Potatoes contains approximately 278 calories and 8 grams of fat (26% of calories from fat).
